description_long: This is a medium-sized barn with a steeply pitched gable roof, set on a rectangular base. The front facade features a central double door with a small overhang, supported by visible wooden beams and trusses. The exterior siding is vertically oriented, giving the structure a classic agricultural appearance. The roof is dark and appears to be made of metal or asphalt panels, extending over the sides for additional weather protection. There are no visible windows on any side, and the rear and side elevations are plain, emphasizing the utilitarian design. The barn sits on a flat platform, suggesting it is intended to be placed in a level urban or rural environment.
description: A classic barn with a steep gable roof, vertical siding, and a central double door, lacking windows and featuring exposed structural beams.
description_view_0: Front view showing the double door entrance, overhang, and exposed wooden beams.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, uninterrupted roof and absence of windows, with three small steps or platforms along the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain gable end with no doors or windows, and visible structural framing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1 with the same roof and base features.
attribute_door_style: double hinged doors with central placement and overhang
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows on any side
